talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
talk_metadata.talk_title: Developing CAR T therapies for T‑cell malignancies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I don't know what to say, really."
Speaker said: "We thought that after seeing a few patients with T‑cell ALL die from no option, we thought that maybe we should try to push this forward."
Speaker said: "The FDA said they were impressed enough that they said proceed with the registration study."
Speaker said: "The FDA was completely fixated on CRS, and they wanted us to be extremely careful."
Speaker said: "We did a simple experiment where we deleted CD2 in a CAR and made the CAR to CD19 as a sort of a control."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Loss of CD7 surface expression on relapsed leukemic blasts (splicing abnormalities, biallelic deletion, down‑regulation) not pre‑emptively addressed
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "We noticed that some of the patients had lost CD7 expression. In fact, most of them that relapsed had lost CD7 expression."
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted immunodeficiency from targeting CD7 and CD2 but did not quantify infection‑related mortality

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Allogeneic CD7‑CAR T for relapsed/refractory T‑cell ALL
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all relapsed T‑cell ALL patients without pre‑screening for CD7 expression or other resistance biomarkers
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline CD7 expression / mechanisms of CD7 loss
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No on‑treatment ctDNA or serial flow cytometry to track CD7 loss

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Multiplex CRISPR editing of CD7 and TRAC in allogeneic donor T cells
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed detailed manufacturing steps (dasatinib early, alpha/beta depletion, OKT3‑mediated cell kill) revealing reliance on a complex gene‑editing workflow that could be circumvented by alternative editing platforms
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Need for alpha/beta affinity column or OKT3‑mediated depletion to achieve <5% residual TRAK+ cells, which reduces overall yield
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without biomarker stratification and reliance on a single antigen (CD7) despite known antigen loss

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that CD2 knockout CAR T cells are functionally impaired in vitro and in vivo, yet later pursued CD2 as a primary target for T‑cell non‑Hodgkin lymphoma without fully resolving the functional deficit

key_data_gaps: Long‑term infection and immunodeficiency outcomes not reported
No biomarker‑stratified efficacy analysis (e.g., CD7 loss kinetics)

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify patients at risk of CD7 antigen loss using CRISPR‑derived resistance signatures and serial ctDNA/flow monitoring
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We noticed that some of the patients had lost CD7 expression. In fact, most of them that relapsed had lost CD7 expression."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genomic, transcriptomic and ctDNA data to predict CD7 loss and guide patient selection or early switch to alternative CARs

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the CD2 costimulatory pathway by epitope‑masked CD2 or CD2‑cytoplasmic domain insertion to overcome CD2‑knockout functional loss
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "When we delete CD2 in T cells and in these CAR T cells, they take a huge hit. We did not see that with CD7."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model CD2‑CAR signaling networks to design optimal intracellular domains that restore function without antigen expression

crispro_opportunity.description: Co‑administer long‑acting IL‑7 (NTi‑7) to boost CAR T expansion and persistence in T‑cell malignancies
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The addition of just a cytokine, IL‑7, provided curative potential in multiple mouse models."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict cytokine‑CAR synergy and optimal dosing windows to maximize expansion while minimizing CRS

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate early IL‑7 dosing (days 1‑7) and prospective CRS mitigation (PI3γδ + JAK inhibition) into the registration protocol
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If you wait 21 days, there's no benefit. If you wait 14 days, there's a diminished benefit, and you have to give it between days one and day seven for them to be markedly improved."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial arms with varied IL‑7 timing and prophylactic kinase inhibition to forecast efficacy and safety outcomes

cited_competitors.name: AbbVie
cited_competitors.context: Sponsor of the AACR award supporting the session

cited_competitors.name: WuJin
cited_competitors.context: Company founded by former postdoc who helped develop the CD7 CAR platform

cited_competitors.name: FDA
cited_competitors.context: Regulatory body imposing restrictions on IL‑7 timing and CRS monitoring

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal timing and dosing schedule for long‑acting IL‑7 in patients receiving UCAR T therapies?
What molecular mechanisms drive CD7 down‑regulation in relapsed patients?
Can CD2‑targeted CARs be engineered to retain function without epitope masking?
